headline: AGNICO EAGLE DROPS C$3.7B TRIPLE-DEAL TO CONSOLIDATE FINLAND'S ENTIRE LAPLAND GOLD BELT
snippet: Agnico Eagle announced three simultaneous deals to acquire Rupert Resources (C$2.9B), Aurion Resources (C$481M all-cash), and B2Gold's 70% stake in Fingold Ventures, consolidating full control of Finland's Central Lapland Greenstone Belt. All three transactions close in Q3 2026, cementing Agnico as the dominant gold producer in northern Europe and providing exit liquidity for two TSX-V juniors.

headline: USA RARE EARTH BUYS BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE FOR $2.8B TO BUILD SUPPLY CHAIN OUTSIDE CHINA
snippet: USA Rare Earth announced a $2.8 billion acquisition of Brazilian rare earths producer Serra Verde, paying $300 million cash and $126.9 million in newly issued stock, with closing expected in Q3 2026. Separate from its Oklahoma magnet line, this is a full upstream-to-manufacturing integration play ahead of anticipated Chinese export restrictions on rare earth products.

headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,804 AS HORMUZ STAYS OPEN AND CENTRAL BANKS KEEP STACKING — UP 39% IN A YEAR
snippet: Gold traded at $4,804 per ounce Monday morning, up 6.58% over the past month and 39.3% year-over-year, as ceasefire developments kept the Strait of Hormuz open and central bank buying continued without pause. JPMorgan maintains conviction on a path toward $5,000, with Goldman flagging a scenario where even modest portfolio rebalancing out of U.S. assets pushes prices to $6,000.

headline: WASHINGTON BACKS $50M EQUITY STAKE IN SOUTH AFRICA RARE EARTH PROJECT DESPITE DIPLOMATIC RIFT
snippet: The U.S. Development Finance Corporation committed a $50 million equity stake in South Africa's Phalaborwa Rare Earths Project, which recovers rare earth elements from industrial mining waste. The investment comes despite a recent diplomatic clash between Washington and Pretoria, signalling that critical mineral supply chain security now trumps bilateral tensions.

headline: GREENLAND APPROVES CRITICAL METALS' 92.5% TANBREEZ STAKE — STOCK SURGES 24% TO $11.46
snippet: The Government of Greenland approved transfer of the remaining 50.5% interest in Tanbreez Mining to Critical Metals Corp., giving the company full operating authority over one of the world's largest undeveloped rare earth resources at 92.5% ownership. Shares surged 23.6% in pre-market trading to $11.46, lifting the company's market cap to approximately $1.4 billion.

headline: RESERVE DEPLETION: THE GOLD SUPPLY CRISIS MAJORS CAN'T FIX WITHOUT THE JUNIORS
snippet: The world's biggest gold producers are drawing down reserves faster than exploration can replace them, with grassroots budgets at historic lows and new discoveries taking 10+ years to reach production. The analysis argues this structural deficit makes district-scale junior acquisitions — not internal drill programs — the only viable path to reserve replacement, with today's Agnico Eagle deal as evidence.

headline: WALL STREET'S DISCOVERY PREMIUM: COPPER-GOLD JUNIORS COMMAND A 40% VALUATION BOOST IN M&A
snippet: A deal-level analysis of recent junior mining M&A shows that companies hosting both copper and gold resources consistently attract a 40%-plus premium over single-metal peers when acquired. The data maps the grade and scale thresholds that trigger strategic buyer interest — directly relevant context as major miners like Agnico Eagle accelerate reserve replacement acquisitions.

headline: CHINA BANS SULPHURIC ACID EXPORTS FROM MAY 1 — COPPER SMELTERS' MARGIN LIFELINE CUT OFF
snippet: China plans to halt sulphuric acid exports from May 1 after Iran war disruptions cut off Middle East sulfur supplies, sending acid prices above $400 per tonne and temporarily ending the Chinese smelters' copper concentrate buying strike. Analysts warn the ban will reverse that reprieve: without by-product acid revenue, Chinese smelters face renewed pressure to cut copper output, tightening global refined supply.
